title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: glasstabs provides animated Shiny widgets tab navigation selection controls: Tabs: glass-morphism tab navigation bar sliding halo, spring easing, luminous transfer trace tabs Multi-select filter: dropdown filter animated checkboxes, live search, tag-pill display, three checkbox styles Single-select dropdown: animated single-select input optional search clear support widgets work plain fluidPage() bs4Dash. can used together independently. —

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
code: # From CRAN  install.packages("glasstabs")  # From GitHub pak::pak("YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/glasstabs")  # From source devtools::install_local("path/to/glasstabs")

what: The one rule: call useGlassTabs() once
title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Every glasstabs app needs exactly one call useGlassTabs() somewhere UI. injects CSS JavaScript proper htmltools dependency.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   # <-- this is all you need   # ... rest of your UI )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: glassTabPanel() takes unique value, display label, UI content:
code: glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,   h3("Welcome"),   p("This is the overview pane.") )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Pass panels glassTabsUI() along namespace id:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       shiny::h3("Welcome"),       shiny::p("Start here.")     ),     glassTabPanel("analysis", "Analysis",       shiny::h3("Analysis"),       shiny::p("Your charts go here.")     ),     glassTabPanel("settings", "Settings",       shiny::h3("Settings"),       shiny::p("Configuration options.")     )   ) )

what: Step 3 — read the active tab in the server
title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: active tab value pushed Shiny automatically every click:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe({     req(input[["nav-active_tab"]])     message("User is on: ", input[["nav-active_tab"]])   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Step 1 — define choices and place the widget
title: Getting Started with glasstabs
code: choices <- c(Alpha = "alpha", Beta = "beta", Gamma = "gamma", Delta = "delta")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("category", choices),   verbatimTextOutput("selected") )

what: Step 2 — read the selection in the server
title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: input$category always plain character vector checked values — use like Shiny input filter data, drive outputs, trigger reactives.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$selected <- renderPrint(input$category) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: want small convenience wrapper:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   ms <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "category")    observe({     message("Selected: ", paste(ms$selected(), collapse = ", "))     message("Style: ", ms$style())   }) }

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() also supports server-side updates:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$reset, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "category",       selected = character(0)     )   }) }

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
code: choices <- c(   North = "north",   South = "south",   East  = "east",   West  = "west" )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassSelect("region", choices, clearable = TRUE),   verbatimTextOutput("selected") )

what: Step 2: read the value in the server
title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: input$region single character value, NULL nothing selected.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$selected <- renderPrint(input$region) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$pick_south, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "region",       selected = "south"     )   }) }

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: common pattern using one filters drive content inside tab panes. Pass widget extra_ui place glassFilterTags() inside panes show active multi-select filters removable tag pills.
code: choices <- c(North = "north", South = "south", East = "east", West = "west")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("main",     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(       inputId             = "region",       choices             = choices,       show_style_switcher = FALSE     ),     glassTabPanel("summary", "Summary", selected = TRUE,       shiny::h3("Summary"),       glassFilterTags("region"),        # tag pills appear here       shiny::uiOutput("summary_text")     ),     glassTabPanel("detail", "Detail",       shiny::h3("Detail"),       glassFilterTags("region"),        # same filter, second pane       shiny::tableOutput("detail_table")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    selected_regions <- reactive({     input$region %||% unique(unname(choices))   })    output$summary_text <- renderUI({     shiny::p("Showing data for: ",              shiny::strong(paste(selected_regions(), collapse = ", ")))   })    output$detail_table <- renderTable({     data.frame(Region = selected_regions())   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: widgets default "dark". Switch "light" supply custom theme object — case override need:
code: # Built-in light preset glassTabsUI("nav",    theme = "light", ...) glassMultiSelect("f", theme = "light", ...)  # Custom — one field each glassTabsUI("nav",   theme = glass_tab_theme(halo_bg = "rgba(251,191,36,0.15)"),   ... )  glassMultiSelect("f", choices,   theme = glass_select_theme(accent_color = "#f59e0b") )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Add wrap = FALSE glass halo positions relative card body rather full-page container. Pair theme = "light":
code: library(bs4Dash) library(glasstabs)  choices <- c(Alpha = "alpha", Beta = "beta", Gamma = "gamma")  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(title = "My App"),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(disable = TRUE),   body    = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),     bs4Card(       title = "Analysis", width = 12,       glassTabsUI("dash",         wrap = FALSE,         theme = "light",         extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(           "f",           choices,           theme = "light",           show_style_switcher = FALSE         ),         glassTabPanel("a", "Overview", selected = TRUE,           shiny::p("Overview content.")         ),         glassTabPanel("b", "Detail",           shiny::p("Detail content.")         )       )     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {} shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Animated Tabs — full reference glassTabsUI(): theming, keyboard nav, multiple instances, server patterns Multi-Select Filter — full reference glassMultiSelect(): checkbox styles, custom hues, tag pills, theming Single-Select Dropdown: search, clearable inputs, explicit “” choices, server-side updates Reference — complete function documentation help(package = "glasstabs")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: glassSelect() animated single-select dropdown Shiny. behaves like standard Shiny input: input$<inputId> stores selected value single character string, NULL nothing selected. supports: optional labels search clear controls built-dark light themes custom theming glass_select_theme() selectable marker styles via check_style server-side updates updateGlassSelect()

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  fruits <- c(   Apple  = "apple",   Banana = "banana",   Cherry = "cherry",   Mango  = "mango" )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: # No selection (default) glassSelect("f", fruits)  # Pre-select one value glassSelect("f", fruits, selected = "banana")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: glassSelect("f", fruits, label = "Choose a fruit")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: glassSelect() supports three selection marker styles: "checkbox" (default) "check-" "filled"
code: glassSelect("f1", fruits, check_style = "checkbox") glassSelect("f2", fruits, check_style = "check-only") glassSelect("f3", fruits, check_style = "filled")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: Search enabled default. can also enable clear control. hide search box:
code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   searchable = TRUE,   clearable = TRUE ) glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   searchable = FALSE )

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: can prepend explicit “” option: useful want UI distinguish : - selection - deliberate “” selection
code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   include_all = TRUE,   all_choice_label = "All fruits",   all_choice_value = "__all__" )

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: Read current value directly input$<inputId>:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe({   message("Selected: ", if (is.null(input$pick)) "NULL" else input$pick) }) }

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: want small convenience wrapper, use glassSelectValue():
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   pick <- glassSelectValue(input, "pick")    observe({     print(pick())   }) }

what: Updating choices and selection from the server
title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: updateGlassSelect() can update: - available choices - current selection - selection marker style follows Shiny-style update semantics: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection choices updated without selected, widget keeps current value still exists new set choices.
code: observeEvent(input$filled, {   updateGlassSelect(     session,     "pick",     check_style = "filled"   ) }) ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   actionButton("subset", "Keep first 2 fruits"),   actionButton("banana", "Select banana"),   actionButton("clear", "Clear"),   actionButton("filled", "Use filled style"),   glassSelect("pick", fruits, clearable = TRUE),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick)    observeEvent(input$subset, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       choices = fruits[1:2]     )   })    observeEvent(input$banana, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = "banana"     )   })    observeEvent(input$clear, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = character(0)     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: glassSelect("f", fruits, theme = "dark") glassSelect("f", fruits, theme = "light")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "dark",     accent_color = "#f59e0b"   ) )  glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     bg_color = "#1a0a2e",     border_color = "#a855f7",     text_color = "#ede9fe",     accent_color = "#a855f7"   ) )   #other themes glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "light",     accent_color = "#2563eb"   ) )  #more themes glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "light",     bg_color = "#ffffff",     border_color = "rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",     text_color = "#111111",     accent_color = "#111111",     label_color = "#111111"   ) )

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: glassSelect() can used without tab widget. need useGlassTabs() load package CSS JavaScript assets:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassSelect("region", c(North = "North", South = "South", East = "East")),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$region) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() animated multi-select dropdown Shiny. can used inside glassTabsUI() layout completely Shiny page. widget behaves like standard Shiny input: input$<inputId> stores selected values, input$<inputId>_style stores active indicator style. also supports server-side updates updateGlassMultiSelect().

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  fruits <- c(Apple = "apple",              Banana = "banana",              Cherry = "cherry",             Mango  = "mango",              Peach  = "peach")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: # All selected (default) glassMultiSelect("f", fruits)  # Subset pre-selected glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, selected = c("apple", "cherry"))  # Nothing pre-selected glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, selected = character(0))

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Three indicator styles available via check_style: default, style switcher row shown inside dropdown user can change style runtime. Hide show_style_switcher = FALSE:
code: # Bordered box + animated tick (default) glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "checkbox")  # Tick only — minimal, no box border glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "check-only")  # Solid coloured box — each option gets its own hue glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "filled") glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   check_style         = "check-only",   show_style_switcher = FALSE          # lock the style silently )

what: Custom hues for the filled style
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: check_style = "filled", hues auto-assigned evenly around colour wheel. Override named integer vector HSL hue angles (0–360):
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   check_style = "filled",   hues = c(apple = 0, banana = 50, cherry = 340, mango = 30, peach = 20) )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: three interactive chrome elements can toggled independently. Defaults TRUE:
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   show_style_switcher = FALSE,   # hide the Check / Box / Fill row   show_select_all     = FALSE,   # hide the "Select all" row   show_clear_all      = FALSE    # hide the "Clear all" footer link )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Place glassFilterTags() container anywhere page show active selections removable tag pills. JS engine keeps sync automatically — clicking × tag deselects option. can place multiple glassFilterTags("cat") containers filter — stay sync.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("cat", fruits, show_style_switcher = FALSE),   glassFilterTags("cat")    # can be placed anywhere, even in a different tab pane )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Read selection directly input$<inputId> style input$<inputId>_style:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    # Direct access   observe({     message("Selected: ", paste(input$pick, collapse = ", "))     message("Style: ",    input$pick_style)   }) }

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: want small convenience wrapper, use glassMultiSelectValue():
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   ms <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "pick")    observe({     message("Selected: ", paste(ms$selected(), collapse = ", "))     message("Style: ", ms$style())   }) }

what: Updating choices and selection from the server
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: updateGlassMultiSelect() can update: - available choices - current selection - active style follows Shiny-style update semantics: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection choices updated without selected, widget keeps intersection current selection new choice set.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   actionButton("subset", "Keep first 3 fruits"),   actionButton("pick2", "Select 2 fruits"),   actionButton("clear", "Clear"),   actionButton("fill", "Switch to filled style"),   glassMultiSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick)    observeEvent(input$subset, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       choices = fruits[1:3]     )   })    observeEvent(input$pick2, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = c("apple", "cherry")     )   })    observeEvent(input$clear, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = character(0)     )   })    observeEvent(input$fill, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       check_style = "filled"     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, theme = "dark")   # default glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, theme = "light")  # white panel, dark text

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Supply colors want change:
code: # One field — accent colour only glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(accent_color = "#f59e0b") )  # Two fields glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     text_color   = "#1e293b",     accent_color = "#2563eb"   ) )  # All four fields glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     bg_color     = "#1a0a2e",     border_color = "#a855f7",     text_color   = "#ede9fe",     accent_color = "#a855f7"   ) )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() fully independent tab widget. need useGlassTabs() CSS JS:
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  sales <- data.frame(   region  = c("North","North","South","South","East","West"),   product = c("Alpha","Beta","Alpha","Gamma","Beta","Alpha"),   revenue = c(42000, 31000, 27000, 55000, 38000, 61000) )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("region",  c(North="North", South="South",                                 East="East",   West="West"),                    show_style_switcher = FALSE),   glassMultiSelect("product", c(Alpha="Alpha", Beta="Beta", Gamma="Gamma"),                    show_style_switcher = FALSE,                    check_style = "check-only"),   glassFilterTags("region"),   glassFilterTags("product"),   tableOutput("tbl") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   filtered <- reactive({     sales[sales$region  %in% (input$region  %||% unique(sales$region)) &           sales$product %in% (input$product %||% unique(sales$product)), ]   })   output$tbl <- renderTable(filtered()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() scoped inputId works independently. one dropdown open automatically floats others:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("a", c(X = "x", Y = "y", Z = "z")),   glassMultiSelect("b", c(P = "p", Q = "q", R = "r"),                    check_style = "filled",                    show_style_switcher = FALSE) )

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: glassTabsUI() provides animated glass-morphism tab navigation bar Shiny. active tab tracked sliding glass halo spring easing, luminous transfer trace sweeps across intermediate tabs navigation.

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Three functions work together: useGlassTabs() — injects CSS JS , anywhere page UI glassTabsUI() — renders full tab bar content area glassTabPanel() — defines one tab button associated content pane
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       h3("Overview"),       p("This pane is shown first.")     ),     glassTabPanel("details", "Details",       h3("Details"),       p("Switch to this tab using the button above.")     ),     glassTabPanel("settings", "Settings",       h3("Settings"),       p("A third tab.")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {}  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Reading the active tab in the server
title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: active tab value pushed Shiny automatically every click via Shiny.setInputValue. Access input[["<id>-active_tab"]]:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe({     req(input[["nav-active_tab"]])     message("Active tab: ", input[["nav-active_tab"]])   })    # Or use the convenience wrapper   active <- glassTabsServer("nav")   observe(message("Active: ", active())) }

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Arrow keys move tabs focus inside widget — extra code needed.

what: Placing a filter widget beside the tabs
title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Pass UI element extra_ui place right tab bar. common use glassMultiSelect() filter:
code: choices <- c(Alpha = "alpha", Beta = "beta", Gamma = "gamma")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect("cat", choices, show_style_switcher = FALSE),     glassTabPanel("a", "Tab A", selected = TRUE,       p("Content A"),       glassFilterTags("cat")   # tag pills synced to the filter     ),     glassTabPanel("b", "Tab B",       p("Content B"),       glassFilterTags("cat")     )   ) )

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
code: # Dark (default) glassTabsUI("nav", theme = "dark", ...)  # Light — suits white page backgrounds and bs4Dash cards glassTabsUI("nav", theme = "light", ...)

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Supply values want change — everything else falls back dark preset: eight handles available glass_tab_theme():
code: # Change only the halo colour glassTabsUI("nav",   theme = glass_tab_theme(     halo_bg         = "rgba(251,191,36,0.18)",     halo_border     = "rgba(251,191,36,0.40)",     tab_active_text = "#fef3c7"   ),   glassTabPanel("a", "Tab", selected = TRUE, p("Content")) )

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Set wrap = FALSE embedding inside bs4Card bs4Box — card body already provides constrained container. Pair theme = "light" match card’s white background:
code: library(bs4Dash) library(glasstabs)  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(title = "My App"),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(disable = TRUE),   body    = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),     bs4Card(       title = "Analysis", width = 12,       glassTabsUI("dash",         wrap     = FALSE,         theme    = "light",         extra_ui = glassMultiSelect("f", choices, theme = "light",                                     show_style_switcher = FALSE),         glassTabPanel("a", "Overview", selected = TRUE, p("Overview content")),         glassTabPanel("b", "Details",  p("Detail content"))       )     )   ) )

what: Multiple tab widgets on one page
title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: glassTabsUI() instance scoped id, multiple widgets work fully independently:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),    # only needed once per page   glassTabsUI("widget1",     glassTabPanel("a", "One-A", selected = TRUE, p("Widget 1, pane A")),     glassTabPanel("b", "One-B", p("Widget 1, pane B"))   ),   glassTabsUI("widget2",     glassTabPanel("x", "Two-X", selected = TRUE, p("Widget 2, pane X")),     glassTabPanel("y", "Two-Y", p("Widget 2, pane Y"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe(message("Widget 1 active: ", input[["widget1-active_tab"]]))   observe(message("Widget 2 active: ", input[["widget2-active_tab"]])) }

title: Authors and Citation
text: George Arthur. Author, maintainer.

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Animated glass-morphism tab navigation multi-select filter widgets R Shiny

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: glasstabs provides animated Shiny widgets built around glass-morphism aesthetic: glassTabsUI() — animated tab bar sliding glass halo follows active tab glassMultiSelect() — multi-select dropdown three selection styles, live search, tag-pill syncing, server-side update support glassSelect() — animated single-select dropdown optional search, clear support, theming, selection styles, server-side update support widgets self-contained, themeable, work plain fluidPage(), bs4DashPage(), Shiny page wrappers. Full documentation: https://prigasg.github.io/glasstabs/ widgets self-contained, fully themeable, work plain fluidPage(), bs4DashPage(), Shiny page wrapper. Full documentation: https://prigasg.github.io/glasstabs/

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
code: # From CRAN install.packages("glasstabs")  # From GitHub (development version) pak::pak("prigasG/glasstabs") # or devtools::install_github("prigasG/glasstabs")

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Note: useGlassTabs() must called somewhere UI glassTabsUI() glassMultiSelect() call. injects shared CSS JavaScript properly deduplicated htmltools dependency.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI(     "main",     glassTabPanel(       "t1", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       h3("Overview"),       p("Content here."),       glassFilterTags("cat")     ),     glassTabPanel(       "t2", "Details",       h3("Details"),       p("More content."),       glassFilterTags("cat")     ),     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(       "cat",       c(A = "a", B = "b", C = "c"),       show_style_switcher = FALSE     )   ),   verbatimTextOutput("info") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   tabs <- glassTabsServer("main")   filt <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "cat")    output$info <- renderPrint({     list(       active_tab = tabs(),       selected = filt$selected(),       style = filt$style()     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Note: default, glassMultiSelect() starts choices selected.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  choices <- c(   Apple  = "apple",   Banana = "banana",   Cherry = "cherry" )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("fruit", choices),   glassFilterTags("fruit"),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$fruit) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$clear, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "fruit",       selected = character(0)     )   })    observeEvent(input$fill_style, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "fruit",       check_style = "filled"     )   }) }

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: ```{r single-select} library(shiny) library(glasstabs) choices <- c( North = “north”, South = “south”, East = “east”, West = “west” ) ui <- fluidPage( useGlassTabs(), glassSelect( “region”, choices, clearable = TRUE, check_style = “checkbox”, theme = “light” ), verbatimTextOutput(“”) ) server <- function(input, output, session) { outputout<−renderPrint(inputout <- renderPrint(inputregion) } shinyApp(ui, server)
code: ## Server-side updates  ```r server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$pick_south, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "region",       selected = "south"     )   })    observeEvent(input$clear_region, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "region",       selected = character(0)     )   })    observeEvent(input$fill_region, {   updateGlassSelect(     session,     "region",     check_style = "filled"   ) }) }

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: widgets default "dark". can switch "light" supply custom theme object.
code: # Tab widget glassTabsUI(   "nav",   glassTabPanel("a", "A", selected = TRUE, p("Content")),   theme = glass_tab_theme(     halo_bg = "rgba(251,191,36,0.15)",     tab_active_text = "#fef3c7"   ) )  # Multi-select glassMultiSelect(   "filter", choices,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "dark",     accent_color = "#f59e0b"   ) )  # Single-select glassSelect(   "region", choices,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "light",     accent_color = "#2563eb"   ) )  # Built-in light preset glassTabsUI("nav", theme = "light", ...) glassMultiSelect("f", choices, theme = "light", ...) glassSelect("s", choices, theme = "light", ...)

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: glassMultiSelect() glassSelect() support three selection indicator styles: Hues distribute automatically around color wheel can set manually multi-select:
code: glassMultiSelect(   "f", choices,   check_style = "filled",   show_style_switcher = FALSE )  glassSelect(   "s", choices,   check_style = "check-only" ) glassMultiSelect("f", c(Apple = "apple", Banana = "banana", Cherry = "cherry"),   check_style = "filled",   hues = c(apple = 10L, banana = 50L, cherry = 340L) )

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Pass wrap = FALSE embedding inside bs4Dash card.
code: bs4Card(   glassTabsUI("dash",     wrap     = FALSE,     theme    = "light",     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect("f", choices, theme = "light"),     glassTabPanel("a", "Overview", selected = TRUE, p("Content")),     glassTabPanel("b", "Details",  p("More"))   ) )

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Multiple glassTabsUI() glassMultiSelect() widgets page work independently — scoped id, CSS variables JS event handlers never bleed across instances.

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets

what: Animated glass multi-select dropdown filter — glassMultiSelect
title: Animated glass multi-select dropdown filter — glassMultiSelect
text: stylized multi-select Shiny input optional search, style switching, select-behavior, programmatic updates via updateGlassMultiSelect().

title: Animated glass multi-select dropdown filter — glassMultiSelect
code: glassMultiSelect(   inputId,   choices,   selected = NULL,   label = NULL,   placeholder = "Filter by Category",   all_label = "All categories",   check_style = c("checkbox", "check-only", "filled"),   show_style_switcher = TRUE,   show_select_all = TRUE,   show_clear_all = TRUE,   theme = "dark",   hues = NULL )

title: Animated glass multi-select dropdown filter — glassMultiSelect
text: inputId Shiny input id. choices Named unnamed character vector choices. selected Initially selected values. Defaults choices NULL. label Optional field label shown widget. placeholder Trigger label nothing selected. all_label Label shown choices selected. check_style One "checkbox" (default), "check-", "filled". show_style_switcher Show Check / Box / Fill switcher row inside dropdown? Default TRUE. show_select_all Show "Select " row? Default TRUE. show_clear_all Show "Clear " footer link? Default TRUE. theme Color theme. One "dark" (default) "light", glass_select_theme() object. hues Optional named integer vector HSL hue angles (0 360) "filled" style. Auto-assigned NULL.

title: Animated glass multi-select dropdown filter — glassMultiSelect
text: htmltools::tagList containing trigger button, dropdown panel, scoped <style> block.

title: Animated glass multi-select dropdown filter — glassMultiSelect
text: widget registers two Shiny inputs: input$<inputId> : character vector selected values input$<inputId>_style : active style string ("checkbox", "check-", "filled") default, selected = NULL, choices initially selected. preserves existing package behavior.

what: Reactive helpers for glassMultiSelect values — glassMultiSelectValue
title: Reactive helpers for glassMultiSelect values — glassMultiSelectValue
text: Convenience helper extracting multi-select widget's value style Shiny's input object without using modules.

title: Reactive helpers for glassMultiSelect values — glassMultiSelectValue
code: glassMultiSelectValue(input, inputId)

title: Reactive helpers for glassMultiSelect values — glassMultiSelectValue
text: input Shiny input object. inputId Input id used glassMultiSelect().

title: Reactive helpers for glassMultiSelect values — glassMultiSelectValue
text: named list two reactives: selected Reactive character vector selected values style Reactive string active style

title: Reactive helpers for glassMultiSelect values — glassMultiSelectValue
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)    ui <- fluidPage(     useGlassTabs(),     glassMultiSelect("cats", c(A = "a", B = "b", C = "c"))   )    server <- function(input, output, session) {     ms <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "cats")     observe({       message("Selected: ", paste(ms$selected(), collapse = ", "))       message("Style: ", ms$style())     })   }    shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Animated glass single-select dropdown — glassSelect
text: stylized single-select Shiny input optional search, clear control, selection-marker styling, programmatic updates via updateGlassSelect().

title: Animated glass single-select dropdown — glassSelect
code: glassSelect(   inputId,   choices,   selected = NULL,   label = NULL,   placeholder = "Select an option",   searchable = TRUE,   clearable = FALSE,   include_all = FALSE,   all_choice_label = "All categories",   all_choice_value = "__all__",   check_style = c("checkbox", "check-only", "filled"),   theme = "dark" )

title: Animated glass single-select dropdown — glassSelect
text: inputId Shiny input id. choices Named unnamed character vector choices. selected Initially selected value. Defaults NULL. label Optional field label shown widget. placeholder Trigger label nothing selected. searchable Logical. Show search input inside dropdown? Default TRUE. clearable Logical. Show clear control removing current selection? Default FALSE. include_all Logical. Prepend explicit "" option. Default FALSE. all_choice_label Label used explicit "" option. all_choice_value Value used explicit "" option. check_style One "checkbox" (default), "check-", "filled". theme Color theme. One "dark" (default) "light", glass_select_theme() object.

title: Animated glass single-select dropdown — glassSelect
text: htmltools::tagList containing single-select trigger, dropdown panel, scoped <style> block.

code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)    ui <- fluidPage(     useGlassTabs(),     glassSelect("fruit", c(Apple = "apple", Banana = "banana"))   )    server <- function(input, output, session) {     fruit <- glassSelectValue(input, "fruit")     observe({       print(fruit())     })   }    shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Define a single glass tab panel — glassTabPanel
title: Define a single glass tab panel — glassTabPanel
text: Used child arguments inside glassTabsUI(). call defines one tab button associated content pane.

title: Define a single glass tab panel — glassTabPanel
code: glassTabPanel(value, label, ..., selected = FALSE)

title: Define a single glass tab panel — glassTabPanel
text: value unique string identifier tab (e.g. ""). label text shown tab button. ... UI elements pane content. selected Logical. Whether tab starts selected. first selected = TRUE tab takes effect; defaults FALSE.

title: Define a single glass tab panel — glassTabPanel
text: list class "glassTabPanel" consumed glassTabsUI().

title: Define a single glass tab panel — glassTabPanel
code:
glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", shiny::h3("Welcome"), shiny::p("This is the overview tab.") ) #> $value #> [1] "overview" #> #> $label #> [1] "Overview" #> #> $content #> $content[[1]] #>

Welcome

#> #> $content[[2]] #>

This is the overview tab.

#> #> #> $selected #> [1] FALSE #> #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "glassTabPanel"

title: Server logic for glass tabs — glassTabsServer
text: id Module id matching id passed glassTabsUI().

title: Server logic for glass tabs — glassTabsServer
text: reactive expression returning active tab value.

title: Server logic for glass tabs — glassTabsServer
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- fluidPage(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI(       "tabs",       glassTabPanel("a", "A", p("Tab A"), selected = TRUE),       glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("Tab B"))     )   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs")     observe(print(active()))   }   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Create a custom color theme for glass select widgets — glass_select_theme
text: mode Base theme preset. One "dark" (default) "light". Custom colors applied top base mode. bg_color Background color trigger button dropdown panel. border_color Border color. text_color Main text color. accent_color Accent color used animated tick, badge, checked-state highlights, clear controls. label_color Optional label color. NULL, label defaults text_color.

title: Create a custom color theme for glass select widgets — glass_select_theme

title: Update a glassMultiSelect widget — updateGlassMultiSelect

title: Update a glassMultiSelect widget — updateGlassMultiSelect
code: updateGlassMultiSelect(   session,   inputId,   choices = NULL,   selected = NULL,   check_style = NULL )

title: Update a glassMultiSelect widget — updateGlassMultiSelect
text: session Shiny session. inputId Input id widget. choices New choices, NULL keep current choices. selected New selected values, NULL keep current selection. Use character(0) clear. check_style Optional new style string. One "checkbox", "check-", "filled". Defaults NULL, keeps current style unchanged.

title: Update a glassMultiSelect widget — updateGlassMultiSelect
text: function now follows Shiny-style update semantics closely: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection choices supplied selected , browser side keeps intersection current selection new set choices.

title: Update a glassSelect widget — updateGlassSelect
text: Update available choices, current selection, /selection-marker style existing glassSelect() input.

title: Update a glassSelect widget — updateGlassSelect
code: updateGlassSelect(   session,   inputId,   choices = NULL,   selected = NULL,   check_style = NULL )

title: Update a glassSelect widget — updateGlassSelect
text: session Shiny session. inputId Input id widget. choices New choices, NULL keep current choices. selected New selected value, NULL keep current selection. Use character(0) clear. check_style Optional new style string. One "checkbox", "check-", "filled". Defaults NULL, keeps current style unchanged.

title: Update a glassSelect widget — updateGlassSelect
text: return value. Called side effect updating client-side widget.

title: Update a glassSelect widget — updateGlassSelect
text: function follows Shiny-style update semantics: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection check_style = NULL leaves current style unchanged choices supplied selected , browser side keeps current selection still present new choices.

what: Attach glasstabs CSS and JS dependencies — useGlassTabs
title: Attach glasstabs CSS and JS dependencies — useGlassTabs
text: Call UI — either inside fluidPage(), bs4DashPage(), Shiny page wrapper. injects required CSS JS proper htmltools dependencies deduplicated automatically.

title: Attach glasstabs CSS and JS dependencies — useGlassTabs
code: useGlassTabs()

title: Attach glasstabs CSS and JS dependencies — useGlassTabs
text: htmltools::htmlDependency object (invisible user, consumed Shiny's renderer).

title: Attach glasstabs CSS and JS dependencies — useGlassTabs
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- fluidPage(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI("demo",       glassTabPanel("A", "Tab A", p("Content A")),       glassTabPanel("B", "Tab B", p("Content B"))     )   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {}   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: glasstabs 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-13

title: glasstabs 0.1.1
text: Added glassSelect(), animated single-select dropdown Shiny. Added updateGlassSelect() server-side updates choices selection. Added glassSelectValue() convenience reactive helper reading selected value.

title: glasstabs 0.1.1
text: Added updateGlassMultiSelect() server-side updates choices, selection, checkbox style. Added glassMultiSelectValue() convenience reactive helper reading selected values active style. Added optional label support glassMultiSelect(). Added configurable all_label support control trigger text shown choices selected.

title: glasstabs 0.1.1
text: Aligned glassSelect() glassMultiSelect() closely standard Shiny input behavior. Added client-side input bindings select widgets now support session$sendInputMessage() update patterns. Preserved current glassMultiSelect() default behavior selected = NULL initializes choices selected. Improved choice normalization named choices retain display labels correctly. Improved hue normalization "filled" multi-select style. Updated vignettes examples show direct Shiny input usage server-side update patterns.

title: glasstabs 0.1.1
text: Added new vignette glassSelect(). Updated getting started vignette include glassSelect(). Updated multi-select vignette document updateGlassMultiSelect() glassMultiSelectValue().

title: glasstabs 0.1.1
text: Added tests glassSelect(), updateGlassSelect(), glassSelectValue(). Expanded tests glassMultiSelect() server-side updates helper utilities.

title: glasstabs 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-11 Initial release.

title: glasstabs 0.1.0
text: glassTabsUI() — animated glassmorphism-style tab navigation sliding glass halo transfer trace tabs. glassTabPanel() — defines single tab button content pane. glassTabsServer() — reactive helper tracks active tab value. glass_tab_theme() — custom color theme glassTabsUI() eight independent handles: tab_text, tab_active_text, halo_bg, halo_border, content_bg, content_border, card_bg, card_text. Built-"dark" (default) "light" presets glassTabsUI(). Keyboard navigation using Left Right arrow keys. Full bs4Dash compatibility via wrap = FALSE.

title: glasstabs 0.1.0
text: glassMultiSelect() — dropdown filter live search, select-indeterminate state, three checkbox indicator styles: "checkbox", "check-", "filled". glassFilterTags() — tag-pill display area stays sync glassMultiSelect() automatically via JavaScript; clicking × pill deselects option. glass_select_theme() — custom color theme four handles: bg_color, border_color, text_color, accent_color. Built-"dark" "light" presets glassMultiSelect(). show_style_switcher, show_select_all, show_clear_all flags hide UI chrome (default TRUE). Per-option HSL hue angles "filled" style via hues argument (auto-assigned omitted).

title: glasstabs 0.1.0
text: useGlassTabs() — injects CSS JavaScript via htmltools::htmlDependency, deduplicated automatically Shiny. theming uses pre-computed CSS variables (color-mix()), ensuring compatibility Shiny’s embedded browser. Multiple instances either widget page work independently; instance scoped id.
